author: y4rr
content:
Surfaced two GPU cascade bugs (cascade_skip references in test):
- #812: flex_attention narrow→add length mismatch on CUDA when batch*heads > 1 with a score_mod closure. The per-(b,h) narrow yields a non-contiguous view; add_inner's CUDA path reads gpu_handle() blindly. Single-(b,h) shapes still work.
- #813: f64 baseline GPU path panics with 'GPU handle does not contain a CudaBuffer<f32>' inside the composed bmm/softmax/mul/reshape chain — one of those device-aware ops is f32-hardcoded for CUDA when handed an f64 tensor.
Both are independent of the conformance test itself (they reproduce on flex_attention's existing API), and both have a documented cascade_skip with a tracking issue ref.
